title: Wire the hybrid retriever into the turn loop (query-aware memory injection)
current_state: pi_agent.py::_prefetch_memory (~line 894) is the only automatic query-time retrieval, and it: fires ONLY on recall-shaped questions (ends with '?' or contains what/which/remind/my...), extracts a SINGLE keyword against a stopword blocklist, and runs BM25 only. get_l3_context injects an importance dump that ignores the query. So on a normal turn the model rarely receives memories relevant to what was actually asked; the strong dense path is never used automatically.
target_state: On turns where cfg.prefetch_memory is set, run MemoryTools.retrieve(user_input, k) and inject the top-k as a compact '[RELEVANT MEMORY]' block (token-budgeted), replacing the single-keyword body of _prefetch_memory. The identity/profile ambient dump (get_l3_context) still provides stable identity facts; query-relevant retrieval supplements it. Episode-recall triggers (T-205) preserved. Net effect: query-aware recall every turn, using dense+BM25, within the context budget — exactly the Track-1 criterion 'recalling critical memories within limited context windows'.
migration_plan: 1. Replace the keyword-extraction body of _prefetch_memory with: results = self.memory.retrieve(user_input, k=6); format a compact block; keep the EPISODE_TRIGGERS branch and the best-effort never-raise wrapper.

2. Keep a token cap on the injected block; keep the recall-signal gate ONLY if it still helps (with real retrieval you may safely retrieve every turn — measure, and default to always-retrieve if cheap).
3. Cache the query embedding for the turn so retrieve() and any tool re-call don't double-embed.

4. Reproducing integration test (the proof + demo evidence): via process_input, state a fact in turn 1 ('we switched the model organism to zebrafish'); in turn 3 ask a PARAPHRASED question ('which animal is the experiment on now?'); assert the retrieved/injected context contains the zebrafish fact — the old single-keyword path missed this.
5. The coherence gate (test_conversation_golden.py) and python scripts/verify.py must stay PASS.
risk_notes: Latency: one embed per turn — mitigate with the per-turn embedding cache; embedding is best-effort so a slow/absent provider degrades to BM25, never blocks the turn. Token budget: the new block must respect the existing L3 budget so we don't blow the context window (the whole point of the tier). Do NOT remove get_l3_context — identity facts (profile) must still be always-on; this ADDS query-relevance, it doesn't replace the profile dump. Coherence gate is the hard constraint: it must stay green.
